title: ProjectConfig
description: Per-project forge configuration. Lives at <project-root>/.forge/project.json. Declares the project's demo shape, quality gate, M2 Studio fields (northStar, instructions, demoProcess, skills, kb, artifactRoot), the release process, and optional acceptance/ci gates, logging knobs, metrics, and sweep plug-ins. The authoritative validator is orchestrator/project-config.ts validateProjectConfig; this schema is the operator-facing reference.

demo.description: How the project surfaces a reviewer-visible demo. The project half of the demo contract (docs/forge-project-contract.md); the forge half is skills/demo/SKILL.md. Demo composition is via demo.shape — there is no per-project demo.skill field.

shape.description: browser = Vite/Playwright; harness = test harness with regex-scrapable output; cli-diff = before/after stdout; artifact = generated file/output; live-external = real resource stood up in a live external system (cloud API + portal), evidence floor is a real REST GET round-trip (liveEvidence.url), not a test-name table; none = infra-only initiatives, DEMO.md rationale block.

command.description: Argv-style command the unifier runs to produce the demo. Required when shape != 'none'.

output.description: Output directory relative to worktree root. Default: demo/<initiative-id>/.

baseline.description: Git ref / branch the unifier diffs against for before/after captures. Default: main.

preview_command.description: Required when shape = 'browser'. The dev/preview server command (forge picks a free PORT).

quality_gate_cmd.description: Argv-style command the developer-loop + unifier run between iterations. Must exit 0 on a green tree. Must be fast and deterministic (see contract clause C1). This is the INNER tier of the two-tier gate model — fast offline unit while iterating.

ci_gate.description: The MERGE tier of the two-tier gate model — the project's FULL CI verification, run directly (operator-trusted, so a `["bash","-c","…&&…"]` chain is allowed) as the final delivery gate before a PR opens. A red ci_gate routes to triage instead of shipping a CI-red PR. Optional.

ci_fix_cmd.description: The project's auto-formatters (e.g. make fmt + make terrafmt), applied best-effort before ci_gate so the PR is fmt-clean. Optional.

ci_gate_unset_env.description: Env-var names STRIPPED from the environment when running ci_gate / ci_fix_cmd, so the CI delivery gate mirrors GitHub CI (a clean env) regardless of the cycle env. Example: ["TF_ACC"] — the serve env sets TF_ACC=1 so per-WI live-acceptance gates run, but the CI gate must run `make test` WITHOUT it (else the whole live suite runs, failing on env GitHub never has). Optional — absent ⇒ env inherited unchanged. See contract A3.

standing_work_item_acs.description: Verbatim acceptance-criterion statements forge appends to EVERY work item the PM emits, as a '## Standing acceptance criteria (project contract)' body section. Encodes project-wide testing invariants (e.g. 'proven by a live acceptance test', 'must not redden CI') as static standing ACs, removing per-WI PM judgment. Optional. See contract A2.

acceptance_gate.description: Live-acceptance-WI requirement (contract C7). For external-resource projects whose behaviour can only be proven against a live system. Optional — absent ⇒ no check.

match.description: Substring that identifies a live-acceptance gate in a WI quality_gate_cmd (any argv token contains it), e.g. 'acceptancetests'.

required.description: When true, the PM phase HARD-FAILS the cycle unless ≥1 emitted WI has a quality_gate_cmd token containing `match` — guaranteeing every initiative is proven by a live acceptance test.

requires_env.description: Env vars that MUST be set for a matching (live-acc) gate to actually run live, e.g. ["TF_ACC"]. If a WI's gate matches `match` but one is unset, the dev-loop ERRORS the gate instead of letting a skipped runner (`ok pkg 0.00s`) false-pass. Optional.

northStar.description: M2 Studio field. One-liner mission statement (≤140 chars) for the project. Flows into the planner's context. Optional.

instructions.description: M2 Studio field. Free-text standing instructions injected into every PM prompt (generalises standing_work_item_acs into prose guidance). Optional.

demoProcess.description: M2 Studio field. Typed demo steps the unifier executes — the executed-demo face of the demo contract (demo.shape is the evidence floor). A capture step under demo.shape 'none' is advisory-incoherent (forge preflight warns). Optional.

kind.description: capture = record real evidence (output/screenshot/API GET); verify = assert the captured evidence against expectations; present = attach the evidence to the PR.

text.description: Human-readable description of the demo step.

skills.description: M2 Studio field. Skill slugs bound to this project (project-action recipes, typically under <project>/forge/skills or .forge/skills). Optional.

kb.description: M2 Studio field. KB id bound to this project, or null to explicitly leave it unbound. Optional (absent ⇒ unset).

artifactRoot.description: Project-root-relative subdirectory under which the project's COMMITTED forge artifacts live: its Brain 3 (<artifactRoot>/brain/) and development history (<artifactRoot>/history/<initiative-id>/). Must be a clean relative path (no leading slash, no '..'). Default '.' keeps the legacy layout (brain/ at the project root); set e.g. 'forge' for a single visible home. Only affects committed artifacts — runtime scratch is unchanged. Optional.

logging.description: Optional logging / heartbeat knobs. Tunes forge-side behaviour without changing forge source.

heartbeat_seconds.description: Cadence in seconds for the agent_heartbeat liveness pulse (default 15s). Tune if the project's SDK calls are unusually long-running.

metrics.description: Optional holistic metrics block. Required for any project that wants exploration-shape initiatives; omit otherwise.

command.description: Argv-style command emitting one or more scalar metrics on stdout.

baselines_dir.description: Directory holding locked baseline markdown files.

tolerance_pct.description: Allowable percentage drift before flagging regression.

sweep.description: Optional parameter-sweep plug-in points. Consumed by skills/project-sweep when the unifier runs in harness-runner mode for type: exploration manifests.

start_command.description: Argv-style command that brings the testbed up.

draw_function.description: Path (worktree-relative) to a module exporting a sample-draw function.

measurement_extractor.description: Path (worktree-relative) to a module that parses metrics.command output.

releaseProcess.description: Optional release-process declaration: the repo-side prep a cycle performs before merge. Mirrors demoProcess — typed, optional, fail-closed when malformed. Tagging the release (git tag) and publishing (npm/registry push) are CI's job, NOT forge step kinds. Absent ⇒ no release steps.

steps.description: Ordered release steps forge performs. Each step is tagged with a kind (docs | changelog | version) and a phase (in-cycle | pre-merge).

kind.description: docs = refresh project docs; changelog = write a changelog entry; version = bump the version file. Tag/publish are CI's job, not a forge step kind.

phase.description: in-cycle = during the dev-loop alongside feature work; pre-merge = after the dev-loop, before the unifier opens the PR.

text.description: Human-readable description of the release step.

command.description: Optional argv-style command forge runs to perform the step.

versionFile.description: Worktree-relative path to the file holding the project version (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').

changelogPath.description: Worktree-relative path to the changelog file (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').

docsDir.description: Worktree-relative directory holding the project's docs (clean relative path — no leading slash, no '..').
